title: Auth constructor errors use inconsistent ClientError variants
description: BearerAuth::new() returns Err(ClientError::InvalidHeaderValue) for invalid characters. BasicAuth::new() returns Err(ClientError::Parse) for the colon-in-username case and Err(ClientError::InvalidHeaderValue) for invalid characters. Callers who pattern-match on the error to display user-facing messages must know which variant to expect from which constructor. The inconsistency (Parse vs InvalidHeaderValue for constructor-time validation failures) is not documented and will generate user issues when callers match only one variant. Maintenance consequence: any change to which variant is returned is a silent breaking change for matching callers.
